title: Hieracium aquaenovae
taxonomy: Hieracium aquaenovae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, commonly known as hawkweeds. The species is native to mountainous regions of Europe. Hieracium aquaenovae is further classified as a member of the subgenus Hieracium section Hieracium. Within this classification, it is often distinguished from other related species based on its unique characteristics.

intro: Hieracium aquaenovae, also known as the watered hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium aquaenovae include sesquiterpene lactones, such as aquaenovin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Hieracium aquaenovae is used in herbal medicine, and it is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
